user_query:
Please set up a small, reproducible CI-like data pipeline in this workspace that builds artifacts about two of my favorite topics. Use the provided input/topics.yaml. For each topic listed there:

1) Perform a web search (use a general search engine, not the target site’s own search) to locate the official Wikidata entity page for the topic by title. Do not hardcode QIDs. Save the raw HTML of the search results page to artifacts/search/<slug>_search.html and record the exact query string used. From the chosen result, capture the Wikidata entity page URL and the QID.

2) Download the entity JSON from the official Wikidata API using the discovered QID and save it to artifacts/raw/wikidata/<QID>.json. Also write a summary mapping file at artifacts/search/found_qids.json containing an array of objects with fields: topic, slug, query, source_url (the Wikidata entity page), and qid.

3) Process each downloaded entity JSON into a normalized JSON array saved at artifacts/processed/entities.json. For each entity, include fields: topic, slug, qid, label_en (English label), description_en (English description), instance_of_qids (array of QIDs for P31 if present), sitelinks_count (integer), and source_url (copied from the mapping file).

4) Validate that description_en contains at least one of the expect_keywords for the topic from input/topics.yaml. Produce a human-readable validation report at artifacts/report.md that, per topic, lists qid, pass/fail, and matched_keywords (or empty if none matched). The validate step should exit non-zero if any topic fails.

5) Generate SHA-256 checksums for all files under artifacts/raw and artifacts/processed and write them to artifacts/checksums.sha256.

Implement this as a local, reproducible pipeline with a Makefile providing targets: fetch (search + download), process, validate, all (runs everything), and clean (removes artifacts/). The pipeline must read input/topics.yaml, must rely on live Internet for both the search step and the API downloads, and must not hardcode QIDs or direct URLs. All outputs must be written under the artifacts/ directory paths specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
